AI Fundamentals · Gotchas

Gemini CLI Essentials and Core Configuration

← Back to Course Package
⚠️ .geminiignore is a filter, not a sandbox
While the CLI skips ignored files during bulk indexing, explicitly referencing an ignored file in a prompt can force the model to attempt a read. If you rely on this for security, you risk accidental data leakage or context injection from sensitive files you assumed were unreachable.
⚠️ The 'rewind' command's partial state recovery
Executing 'rewind' successfully reverts the conversation history but frequently fails to roll back physical file system changes made by the agent. This creates a state mismatch where the model believes a file is in its original state while the disk contains the modified version, leading to broken builds or logic errors.
⚠️ WSL 2 OAuth 'Headless' Deadlock
The CLI's automated authentication flow often hangs in WSL 2 environments because it cannot trigger the Windows-native browser for the OAuth callback. Users who wait for a prompt will experience a silent timeout, requiring a manual URL copy-paste to a host browser to prevent authentication failure.
⚠️ Policy Engine Priority Inversion
In the TOML configuration, specific tool rules like 'write_file' require high priority values (e.g., 100) to override default safety or confirmation prompts. Setting a low priority or omitting the priority field will cause the CLI to default to 'deny' or 'prompt', breaking automated CI/CD pipelines and non-interactive scripts.
